dependencies.relationship: Uses CLI infrastructure: Cobra root command, config loading/saving, output formatting, error handling, persistent flags, NewAuthenticatedClient, and help --json metadata registration.

All commands delegate to Go SDK client methods: GetProviders, ExchangeOAuthCode, GetUser, UpdateUser, ListKeys, RefreshKey, RevokeKey, ListTokens, CreateToken, GetToken, RevokeToken, ListUserOrgs, GetOrg, ListOrgMembers.

subtasks.title: Implement parseKeyID helper
details: Create internal/cli/helpers.go.
Implement func parseKeyID(apiKey string) (string, error).
Split on '_'; if len(parts) < 3 return '', fmt.Errorf("invalid API key format").
Return parts[len(parts)-2] as key_id.
Handles prefixes with embedded underscores correctly by always taking penultimate segment.

subtasks.title: Implement validateExpires helper
details: In helpers.go, implement func validateExpires(v int) error.
Return nil if v in {0, 30, 60, 90}.
Return fmt.Errorf("--expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90") for all other values.

subtasks.title: Implement parsePermissions helper
details: In helpers.go, implement func parsePermissions(s string) ([]string, error).
Split s on ','; trim whitespace from each entry; discard empty entries.
If resulting slice is empty, return nil, fmt.Errorf("--permissions must not be empty").
Return non-empty []string, nil on success.
Do NOT validate individual entry format (e.g., resource_type:action).

subtasks.title: Implement openBrowser helper
details: In helpers.go, implement func openBrowser(url string) error.
Use runtime.GOOS: 'darwin' → exec.Command("open", url).Run(); 'linux' → exec.Command("xdg-open", url).Run().
Return the command's error (or a 'unsupported OS' error for other GOOS values).
Extract exec invocation via an injectable function pointer for testability: e.g., var execCommand = exec.Command.

subtasks.title: Implement state generation and authorization URL builder helpers
details: In login.go (or helpers.go), implement func generateState() (string, error): crypto/rand.Read(make([]byte,32)); hex.EncodeToString; return 64-char hex string.

Implement func buildAuthURL(authorizeURL, redirectURI, state string) (string, error): url.Parse authorizeURL; q := u.Query(); add redirect_uri, state, response_type=code; u.RawQuery = q.Encode(); return u.String().
Do NOT add client_id; preserve all existing query params from authorizeURL.

checks: Helper unit tests pass: go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestParseKeyID|TestValidateExpires|TestParsePermissions|TestOpenBrowser|TestGenerateState|TestBuildAuthURL'
go vet ./internal/cli/... reports no issues
Requirements 15-REQ-15.1, 15-REQ-15.2, 15-REQ-16.1, 15-REQ-17.1, 15-REQ-17.2, 15-REQ-18.1, 15-REQ-2.4, 15-REQ-2.6 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement NewLoginCmd and runLogin skeleton with flags and precondition checks
details: Create internal/cli/login.go.
Define const loginTimeoutSeconds = 120.
Define type loginOpts struct { provider string; expires int; endpointURL string }.
Implement func NewLoginCmd() *cobra.Command with --provider (default 'github') and --expires (default 90) flags.
Set help-json annotation: composite=true, method=null, path=null, auth='none'.
RunE: validate endpoint_url present (exit code 2 if missing); call validateExpires (exit code 2 if invalid); call runLogin(ctx, time.Duration(loginTimeoutSeconds)*time.Second, opts).
Implement func runLogin(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration, opts loginOpts) error as a stub (panic("not implemented")) initially.

subtasks.title: Implement OAuth callback server and channel-based communication
details: Implement local callback server: net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0"); &http.Server{Handler: mux}.
Register handler for /callback only; all other paths: w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound); return.

Success HTML const: '

Login successful

You may close this tab.

'.

Error HTML const: '

Login failed

OAuth state mismatch. Please try again.

'.
Handler: validate state param; on mismatch send on errCh, write HTTP 400 + error HTML, return; on match write HTTP 200 + success HTML, send code on codeCh.
Derive redirect_uri as 'http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback' from listener address.

subtasks.title: Implement runLogin core flow: GetProviders, browser open, select, code exchange
details: In runLogin: apikit.NewClient(opts.endpointURL); client.GetProviders(ctx); find provider by name; exit code 1 if not found.
Generate state via generateState(); start callback server; build auth URL via buildAuthURL().
Print 'Opening browser for authentication...' to stderr; call openBrowser(authURL); on failure print 'Open this URL in your browser: <url>' to stderr.
Create codeCh chan string and errCh chan error; create context with timeout using time.WithTimeout.
Select on codeCh (proceed to exchange), errCh (cancel ctx, return CSRF error), ctx.Done() (return timeout error).
On code: call client.ExchangeOAuthCode(ctx, &apikit.AuthCallbackRequest{Provider, Code, RedirectURI, Expires: &opts.expires}).

subtasks.title: Implement runLogin config save, output, and graceful server shutdown
details: On successful ExchangeOAuthCode: call CLI Core save function with endpoint_url, user_id=response.User.ID, api_key=response.APIKey.Key.
On config save failure: return error 'failed to save config: <detail>'; do NOT print user JSON.
On config save success: marshal response.User as indented JSON (two-space, no HTML escape) to stdout; print 'Logged in as <username>' to stderr.
Shutdown: shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second); defer cancel(); server.Shutdown(shutdownCtx).
Always shut down server (defer or explicit) regardless of success/failure path.

checks: Login tests pass: go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestLogin'
go vet ./internal/cli/... reports no issues
Requirements 15-REQ-2.1 through 15-REQ-2.16, 15-REQ-2.E1–2.E5, 15-REQ-22.1 acceptance criteria met
loginTimeoutSeconds == 120 constant defined and never modified

subtasks.title: Implement akc user show and akc user update commands
details: Create internal/cli/user.go.
Implement NewUserCmd() *cobra.Command: parent command 'user' with no RunE.
Implement NewUserShowCmd(): no flags; RunE: NewAuthenticatedClient; client.GetUser(ctx); marshal response.Data as indented JSON to stdout.

Implement NewUserUpdateCmd(): --full-name (string, MarkFlagRequired); no client-side validation; client.UpdateUser(ctx, &apikit.UpdateUserRequest{FullName: fullName}); marshal *User as indented JSON to stdout.
Set help-json annotations: auth='api_key', method='GET'/'PATCH', appropriate paths.
Register show and update as subcommands of user parent.

subtasks.title: Implement akc keys list command
details: Create internal/cli/keys.go.
Implement NewKeysCmd() *cobra.Command: parent command 'keys'.
Implement NewKeysListCmd(): no flags; RunE: NewAuthenticatedClient; client.ListKeys(ctx); marshal []*APIKeyMeta as indented JSON to stdout.
Set help-json annotations: auth='api_key', method='GET'.

subtasks.title: Implement akc keys refresh command
details: Implement NewKeysRefreshCmd(): no flags.
RunE: NewAuthenticatedClient (exits code 2 if api_key absent); parseKeyID(apiKey) (exits code 2 on invalid format).
Call client.RefreshKey(ctx, keyID); on success call CLI Core atomic write to update api_key with response.Key.
On config write failure: return error 'failed to save config: <detail>'; do NOT print new key.
On success: marshal APIKeyFull as indented JSON to stdout; print 'API key refreshed' to stderr.

subtasks.title: Implement akc keys revoke command
details: Implement NewKeysRevokeCmd(): no flags.
RunE: NewAuthenticatedClient; parseKeyID(apiKey) (exits code 2 on invalid format).
Call client.RevokeKey(ctx, keyID); on success clear api_key and user_id to empty strings via CLI Core atomic write.
Marshal RevokeKeyResponse as indented JSON to stdout; print 'API key revoked. Run akc login to obtain a new key.' to stderr.
Register list, refresh, revoke as subcommands of keys parent.

subtasks.title: Implement akc tokens show and tokens revoke commands

Implement NewTokensRevokeCmd(): Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1); client.RevokeToken(ctx, args[0]) returns only error; on success write '{}' to stdout; print 'Token <token_id> revoked' to stderr.

Set help-json annotations on all token commands: auth='api_key', appropriate methods and paths.

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification — End-to-End Integration and Stub Audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths — verify each path's function chain is wired end-to-end
details: 15-PATH-1 (login): verify main.go → NewLoginCmd → RunE → runLogin → GetProviders → callback server → ExchangeOAuthCode → CLI Core save → stdout JSON → stderr message; each step calls the next with no stubs.
15-PATH-2 (user show): verify NewUserShowCmd → RunE → NewAuthenticatedClient → client.GetUser → marshal response.Data → stdout.
15-PATH-3 (keys refresh): verify NewKeysRefreshCmd → RunE → parseKeyID → client.RefreshKey → CLI Core atomic write → stdout APIKeyFull JSON.
Confirm all five parent commands (NewLoginCmd, NewUserCmd, NewKeysCmd, NewTokensCmd, NewOrgsCmd) are called in cmd/akc/main.go.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation — callers receive and use return values
details: Verify runLogin returns error from ExchangeOAuthCode and propagates it to RunE.
Verify NewKeysRefreshCmd uses response.Key from RefreshKey to update config (not ignoring the return value).
Verify NewTokensRevokeCmd writes '{}' to stdout — confirm the hardcoded empty object is used, not the (missing) SDK response body.
Verify CLI Core atomic write return value is checked; error is returned to RunE; not silently dropped.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: TS-15-SMOKE-1: run full login OAuth flow with httptest.Server and simulated browser callback; confirm config saved, stdout user JSON, stderr 'Logged in as', exit 0.
TS-15-SMOKE-2: run akc user show against mock server; confirm GetUser called with auth header, stdout User JSON, exit 0.
TS-15-SMOKE-3: run akc keys refresh; confirm parseKeyID extracts key_id, RefreshKey called, config updated, exit 0.
TS-15-SMOKE-4: run runLogin with 100ms timeout and no callback; confirm timeout error and exit 2.
TS-15-SMOKE-5: run akc tokens create with --permissions 'users:read,orgs:read' --expires 30; confirm CreateToken body correct, PATFull in stdout.
